title: bumelia strigosa
intro: Bumelia strigosa, also known as the striped buckthorn or striped bumelia, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Its bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Bumelia strigosa can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
